events.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 13 of US-Israel attacks?
events.description: Cyberattacks, tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and rising oil prices are deepening the global consequences of the war.

events.title: Southeast Asia shuts offices, limits travel as oil crisis deepens
events.description: Countries such as Indonesia and Vietnam heavily depend on fuel imports and have limited energy reserves.

events.title: France walks ‘fine line’ as US-Israel war on Iran escalates
events.description: President Macron has criticised the onslaught and questioned the war's legality but ultimately blamed Iran.

events.title: Analysis: The war on Iran is at a crossroads
events.description: Iran is raising the costs of a continued war, for the US and Gulf nations. But could it — and the US — escalate further?

events.title: ‘A US military victory in Iran without a political one is not a victory’
events.description: ‘This is not a war about whose military is better, bigger or more powerful.’

events.title: In the US-Israeli war on Iran, who will run out of missiles first?
events.description: Are there enough interceptor missiles to defeat Iran’s response to the US and Israel's attacks?

events.title: Trump admin estimates US war on Iran cost $11.3bn in first 6 days: Report
events.description: Lawmakers express concerns as Trump officials project $50bn more may be needed for Iran war funding.

events.title: Iran targets Gulf nations with missiles, drones as oil prices soar
events.description: Bahrain reports an attack on fuel tanks as Saudi Arabia shoots down drones headed towards an oilfield.

events.title: There is only one person’ who can decide to end the war on Iran
events.description: Israeli journalist and writer, Gideon Levy, says the decision to end the Iran war rests only with US President Trump.

events.title: Why a historic release of oil reserves may not tame surging prices
events.description: Oil prices continue to soar despite the International Energy Agency's plans to release 400m barrels into the market.

events.title: Six vessels attacked amid reports of Iranian drone boats, sea mines
events.description: Iraq reportedly suspends oil terminal operations after two tankers hit by explosive-laden boats in Iraqi waters.

events.title: How is feminism being used to justify war in Iran?
events.description: Some supporters of the US-Israeli war on Iran are using the treatment of women in the country as a justification.

events.title: Fuel tank fire rages in Bahrain after Iranian attack
events.description: Bahrain's interior ministry released footage of a massive blaze at a fuel storage facility following an Iranian attack.

events.title: UN Security Council adopts resolution condemning Iran’s attacks in the Gulf
events.description: UN Security Council draft resolution demanding Iran end its attacks on Gulf nations was cosponsored by 135 countries.

events.title: Who bombed the Iranian girls’ school, killing more than 170? What we know
events.description: The attack on Shajareh Tayyebeh Elementary School quickly became a focal point of concern over civilian casualties.

events.title: Iran’s authorities warn against protests as Israel threatens Basij forces
events.description: Police chief says protesters seen as acting at the behest of the US and Israel will be treated as enemies.

events.title: Qatar PM urges resilience and unity amid Iran strikes
events.description: Qatar’s Prime Minister calls for strengthening the country’s ability to withstand “hardship” amid regional war.

events.title: Bowen: Trump has called for an Iran uprising but the lessons from Iraq in 1991 loom large
events.description: The US president might learn that starting wars is much easier than ending them, writes the BBC's international editor.

events.title: 'Even under missiles we carry on living' - how young Iranians are coping with war
events.description: Iranians say they are sheltering at home and rarely venturing out on near-empty streets as the US-Israeli bombing campaign continues.

events.title: Explosions reported on two foreign vessels in the Gulf, authorities say
events.description: Pressure has intensified on the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most important shipping lanes, since Israel and the US attacked Iran last month.

events.title: EU and UK demand Israel stop surge in West Bank settler violence since Iran war
events.description: Six Palestinians have been killed during attacks by settlers in the West Bank since the start of Israel's war, the UN says.

events.title: US strikes minelaying ships in Iran after warning of 'intense' day of bombing
events.description: US intelligence assets believe Iran is preparing to deploy naval mines in the Strait of Hormuz, the world's busiest oil shipping channel.

events.title: Iranian minister says country will not play in World Cup
events.description: Iran is not in a position to participate in the 2026 World Cup, says the country's Minister of Sports and Youth.

events.title: Member of Iranian football delegation granted Australian asylum changes her mind
events.description: One of the seven women who received visas has reversed her decision after speaking to teammates, minister says

events.title: Inside the Australian hotel where Iranian footballers escaped to claim asylum
events.description: Activists played a key role in making contact with the women as they tried to dodge their minders.

events.title: War expands to central Beirut as Israeli strike kills Iranians in luxury hotel
events.description: Israel's military says it targeted Quds Force operatives after a return to war with Lebanon's Hezbollah movement, but Iran says they were diplomats.

events.title: Why air strikes on Tehran oil facilities are causing black rain
events.description: Air strikes have damaged at least four oil facilities since US-Israeli attacks on Iran began last month.

events.title: 'I just want to be able to sleep': Attacks in Iran rock cities and cut power
events.description: Iranians in Tehran and Karaj tell the BBC they are exhausted and struggling to sleep after 10 days of Israeli and US attacks.

events.title: Strikes shake Iran cities as crowds rally in support of Mojtaba Khamenei
events.description: Iran and Israel continue to exchange attacks, as supporters of Khamenei celebrate the decision to name him supreme leader.

events.title: US missile hit military base near Iran school, video analysis shows
events.description: A US Tomahawk missile hit a military base near a primary school in southern Iran where Iranian authorities said 168 people were killed, expert video analysis shows.

events.title: G7 to take 'necessary measures' to support energy supplies
events.description: Finance ministers and the IEA discussed options for stabilising oil prices which surpassed $100.

events.title: 'Mind boggles' at doubts over legality of Iran war, Israeli president says
events.description: Isaac Herzog says Israel is acting out of "self-defence" on behalf of Israel, the wider region and Europe.

events.title: Mixed messages from Trump leave more questions than answers over war's end
events.description: The US president has tried to soothe nerves over oil and markets, but his comments still lack clarity, writes Anthony Zurcher.

events.title: GPS jamming: The invisible battle in the Middle East
events.description: GPS jamming has made navigation hazardous in the Gulf, spurring efforts to develop alternatives.

events.title: Iran's new leader has never been tested. He now faces an existential battle
events.description: Mojtaba Khamenei, 56, enters power as the Islamic republic faces its most serious crisis.

events.title: In maps: 12 days of strikes across the Middle East
events.description: The US says it "eliminated" 16 Iranian ships as Israel carried out further attacks in Lebanon and Iran.

events.title: Iranians deeply divided over Mojtaba Khamenei's rise to power
events.description: While pro-establishment crowds celebrate Khamenei's appointment as his father's successor, others believe it signals no change to how Iran is ruled.

events.title: 'Night turned into day': Iranians tell of strikes on oil depots
events.description: Fuel depots were hit in Tehran and Karaj overnight, with videos showing huge fires and plumes of smoke in multiple locations.

events.title: Who is Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new supreme leader?
events.description: Many expect the 56-year-old, who has largely kept a low profile, to continue his father's hardline policies.

events.title: Why did Iran's president apologise?
events.description: The apology may hint at a political repositioning inside Iran, writes BBC Persian's Amir Azmi.

events.title: We've been speaking to Iranians during one week of war. Here's what they said
events.description: Ordinary Iranians reflect on seven days of conflict and where they see their country going next.

events.title: The final voyage of the Iranian warship sunk by the US
events.description: The sinking of an Iranian ship by the US near Sri Lanka brings the conflict uncomfortably close to India.

events.title: Israeli troops push into Lebanon for yet another war with Iran's proxy Hezbollah
events.description: The BBC sees troops and tanks massing near the border, as speculation mounts that Israel is planning a full-scale ground invasion to fight Hezbollah.

events.title: Trump wants to pick Iran's new leader - will a hostile regime under fire agree?
events.description: The president's vision of Iran's future could meet fierce opposition, writes chief international correspondent Lyse Doucet.

events.title: Iran's high-risk war strategy seems to centre on endurance and deterrence
events.description: Tehran's approach appears to rest on a belief it can absorb strikes longer than its adversaries sustain pain and costs, writes BBC Persian's Amir Azimi.

events.title: 'We have been preparing': Why the boots on the ground in Iran could be Kurdish
events.description: Iranian Kurdish opposition groups in exile in northern Iraq tell the BBC they have plans to cross the border but deny already doing so.

events.title: 'All red lines have been crossed': Gulf states weigh response to Iranian strikes
events.description: Retaliatory Iranian strikes on Gulf states have tarnished their image as safe and prosperous, writes the BBC's Barbara Plett-Usher.

events.title: Battered and isolated, Hezbollah drags Lebanon into another war
events.description: Its attack on Israel in support of Iran has led to fresh mass displacements and Israeli bombing, to the fury of many Lebanese.

events.title: 'Kill the regime but not people': BBC speaks to Iranians crossing into Turkey
events.description: Dan Johnson hears from Iranians at a crossing in north-west Iran about their thoughts on the war and regime.

events.title: Video released by US shows strikes on Iranian vessels near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Video released by US shows strikes on Iranian vessels near Strait of Hormuz

events.title: Strait of Hormuz timelapse shows fall in shipping traffic
events.description: The footage demonstrates the flow of ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz in the Gulf since the US-Israeli military offensive began against Iran on 27 February.

events.title: BBC visits key Dubai port in Iran's firing line
events.description: Trade at the Middle East's biggest port has been hit hard since Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz began.

events.title: Trump says Iran war will be over 'very soon', but not this week
events.description: At his press briefing, the US president claimed the operation was a "tremendous success", but added that he wants to ensure Iran cannot develop nuclear weaponry.

events.title: Watch: Why the Iran war is pushing up oil prices
events.description: As turmoil continues in the Middle East, the price of crude oil rose to more than $100 a barrel on Monday.

events.title: Kuwaiti government high-rise hit by Iranian drone
events.description: Footage shows flames tearing through the building in the early hours of Sunday, following more attacks on the gulf state.

events.title: Watch: Huge flames in Tehran after air strikes on oil depots
events.description: Social media footage shows flames leaping into the air and plumes of smoke above the city's skyline.
